pairs.question: What were the titles held by Charles I and IV during his rule over Austria-Hungary?
pairs.answer: Charles I and IV held the titles of Emperor of Austria (as Charles I) and King of Hungary (as Charles IV) during his rule over Austria-Hungary. He also ruled over the other states of the Habsburg monarchy from November 1916 until the monarchy was abolished in November 1918.

pairs.question: How did Charles I and IV become the heir presumptive to Emperor Franz Joseph?
pairs.answer: Charles became the heir presumptive of Emperor Franz Joseph when his uncle, Archduke Franz Ferdinand of Austria, was assassinated in 1914.

pairs.question: What were Charles I and IV's efforts to preserve the Austro-Hungarian Empire during World War I?
pairs.answer: Charles I and IV began secret negotiations with the Allies to peacefully end World War I but was unsuccessful. He also attempted to preserve the empire by returning it to federalism and by championing Austro-Slavism.

pairs.question: What actions marked the dissolution of the Austro-Hungarian monarchy in 1918?
pairs.answer: The dissolution of the Austro-Hungarian monarchy in 1918 was marked by the proclamation of Czechoslovakia and the State of Slovenes, Croats, and Serbs, as well as Hungary breaking monarchic ties to Austria by the end of October 1918. Following the Armistice of 11 November 1918, the Republic of German-Austria was proclaimed the following day, and in April 1919, the National Assembly formally dethroned the Habsburgs and banished Charles from German-Austria for life.

pairs.question: What were the circumstances surrounding Charles I and IV's first exile in 1919?
pairs.answer: After renouncing any participation in government affairs following the Armistice of 11 November 1918, Charles I and IV spent the early part of his exile in Switzerland in 1919. Later that year, the National Assembly formally dethroned the Habsburgs and banished him from German-Austria for life.

pairs.question: What attempts did Charles I and IV make to restore the monarchy after his exile?
pairs.answer: Charles I and IV made two attempts to reclaim the Hungarian throne in 1921, but both failed due to opposition from Hungary's Calvinist regent, Admiral Miklós Horthy. He was then exiled for a second time to the Portuguese island of Madeira, where he died in 1922.

pairs.question: How was Charles I and IV recognized by the Catholic Church after his death?
pairs.answer: Charles I and IV was beatified by the Catholic Church in 2004, with October 21 designated as his feast day.
